pyMediaManager
  • API Reference
  • Application Services
  • View page source

Application Services

Project Service

Project service for managing projects.

This module provides the ProjectService class for creating, loading, saving, and managing media management projects.

class app.services.project_service.ProjectService[source]

Bases: object

Service for managing project lifecycle.

Handles project creation, loading, saving, deletion, and listing. Projects are stored as JSON files in the projects directory.

__init__(projects_dir)[source]

Initialize the project service.

Parameters:

projects_dir (Path) – Directory where project metadata files are stored

create_project(name, path, description=None, use_template=None)[source]

Create a new project.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – Project name

  • path (Path) – Project directory path

  • description (str | None) – Optional project description

  • use_template (str | None) – Optional template name to use

Returns:

Created Project instance

Raises:

ValueError – If project already exists or path is invalid

Return type:

Project

load_project(project_path)[source]

Load a project from its directory.

Parameters:

project_path (Path) – Path to the project directory

Returns:

Project instance or None if not found

Return type:

Project | None

save_project(project)[source]

Save project metadata.

Parameters:

project (Project) – Project to save

delete_project(project, delete_files=False)[source]

Delete a project.

Parameters:

  • project (Project) – Project to delete

  • delete_files (bool) – Whether to delete project files (default: False)

list_projects()[source]

List all known projects.

Returns:

List of Project instances

Return type:

list[Project]

Git Service

Git service for managing Git operations.

This module provides a GitService class that wraps GitPython to provide Git functionality for projects.

class app.services.git_service.GitService[source]

Bases: object

Service for Git repository operations.

Provides methods for: - Repository initialization - Status checking - Committing changes - Pushing to remotes

static init_repository(path, initial_commit=True)[source]

Initialize a Git repository.

Parameters:

  • path (Path) – Path to the directory to initialize

  • initial_commit (bool) – Whether to create an initial commit

Returns:

Git Repo object

Raises:

GitCommandError – If Git initialization fails

Return type:

Repo

static get_repository(path)[source]

Get a Git repository at the specified path.

Parameters:

path (Path) – Path to the repository

Returns:

Repo object or None if not a Git repository

Return type:

Repo | None

static is_repository(path)[source]

Check if a directory is a Git repository.

Parameters:

path (Path) – Path to check

Returns:

True if it’s a Git repository

Return type:

bool

static get_status(path)[source]

Get the status of a Git repository.

Parameters:

path (Path) – Path to the repository

Returns:

Dictionary with status information or None if not a repo

Return type:

dict[str, Any] | None

static commit(path, message, add_all=False, files=None)[source]

Commit changes to the repository.

Parameters:

  • path (Path) – Path to the repository

  • message (str) – Commit message

  • add_all (bool) – Whether to stage all changes before committing

  • files (list[str] | None) – Specific files to stage (if add_all is False)

Returns:

True if commit was successful

Raises:

GitCommandError – If commit fails

Return type:

bool

static push(path, remote='origin', branch=None)[source]

Push commits to a remote repository.

Parameters:

  • path (Path) – Path to the repository

  • remote (str) – Name of the remote (default: “origin”)

  • branch (str | None) – Branch to push (default: current branch)

Returns:

Tuple of (success, message)

Return type:

tuple[bool, str]

static add_remote(path, name, url)[source]

Add a remote to the repository.

Parameters:

  • path (Path) – Path to the repository

  • name (str) – Name of the remote

  • url (str) – URL of the remote

Returns:

True if remote was added successfully

Return type:

bool

static get_remotes(path)[source]

Get list of remotes for the repository.

Parameters:

path (Path) – Path to the repository

Returns:

List of dictionaries with remote information

Return type:

list[dict[str, Any]]

static get_log(path, max_count=10)[source]

Get commit log for the repository.

Parameters:

  • path (Path) – Path to the repository

  • max_count (int) – Maximum number of commits to retrieve

Returns:

List of commit dictionaries

Return type:

list[dict[str, Any]]
